q01.question: Which of the following statements regarding spanning trees is true (choose all that apply)?

responses.spanning: In a spanning tree, there is path between any two nodes in the graph.
responses.acyclic: Spanning trees are both connected and acyclic.
responses.single: There is usually only one way to form a spanning tree.
responses.minimum: A minimum spanning tree is a spanning tree that has the least amount of weight as possible.
responses.maximum: A maximum spanning tree is a spanning tree that has the most amount of edges as possible.
responses.unique: There is usually only one unique minimum spanning tree in a graph.
responses.greedy: We can use greedy algorithms to construct minimum spanning trees.
responses.priority: To implement Prim's algorithm, we can use a priority queue.

q02.question: Given the following graph, which of the following orders are topological sorts (choose all that apply)?

responses.one: A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H
responses.two: C, A, B, E, D, F, G, H
responses.three: A, B, C, E, D, H, G, F
responses.four: B, A, D, C, H, E, G, F
responses.five: A, D, H, F, G, E, B, C
responses.six: C, B, E, A, D, H, F, G
responses.seven: A, B, D, F, C, E, G, H
responses.eight: B, A, D, F, C, G, H, E
